content.json:
## Microsoft 365 Activity — User vs Organisation (Model B)

> ⚠️ **Observable telemetry only.** These figures are **not** a measure of productivity, work quality, effort, or hours worked. A higher count does not mean more work was done. Outliers are **prompts to review**, never proof of underperformance or misconduct. The primary model is each user vs their *own* baseline; this view is supporting context only.

This workbook compares each user's per-workload activity against the organisation using **robust statistics** (median, MAD, percentiles, modified z-score), so the comparison isn't skewed by power users, automation, or service accounts. Service accounts, system principals, and B2B guests are excluded.

parameters.name: W_ReportPeriod
parameters.label: Report period
parameters.jsonData: [{"value": "7d", "label": "Last 7 days"}, {"value": "14d", "label": "Last 14 days"}, {"value": "30d", "label": "Last 30 days", "selected": true}, {"value": "60d", "label": "Last 60 days"}, {"value": "90d", "label": "Last 90 days"}]

parameters.name: W_MinUserActivity
parameters.label: Min activity (events) to include a user

content.json:
### Organisation baseline per workload
What “normal” looks like across the org for each workload over **{W_ReportPeriod}**: the median per-day event rate, the P25–P90 spread, and MAD (robust spread). `Users` is how many people the stats rest on — small counts are less reliable. These are the reference figures the table below compares each user against.

### User vs organisation
Each row places a user's per-workload rate against the org distribution above. **Read `PercentileBand` first** (where they sit vs peers); `ModifiedZ` is the robust statistical distance from the median. `OrgFlag` highlights rows where |modified z| > **{W_OutlierZ}** — amber means *worth a look*, not a verdict. Use the column filters to focus on a workload or on outliers. Different roles legitimately produce different bands.
